gloss:
In a four-observable scenario, no obstructing family exists — one that is locally consistent and everywhere nonempty yet fails to glue into a global object — exactly when the cover of questions is acyclic. Whether the local pieces can fail to determine the global object is thereby fixed by the shape of the questioning pattern alone.

**Cites** `WMSpec.Transform.ct5c_acyclic_iff_no_phantom` — the corpus theorem this result restates and discharges.

**Original contribution.** The corpus gives the four-observable equivalence; the thesis-layer statement recasts the non-existence of obstructions as fully determined by the cover's geometry, tying the theory's foundation to the interrogation shape alone.

statement.pretty: ∀ (cover : Finset (Finset (Fin 4))), HiddenChannelCapacity.Acyclic cover ↔ ∀ (fam : List (HiddenChannelCapacity.LocalStruct (Fin 4) fun x => ZMod 2)), List.map (fun x => HiddenChannelCapacity.LocalStruct.team x) fam = Finset.toList cover → HiddenChannelCapacity.Consistent fam → (∀ L ∈ fam, Finset.Nonempty (HiddenChannelCapacity.LocalStruct.rel L)) → HiddenChannelCapacity.Glues fam

title: Eidometry.Thesis.phantom_dichotomy
